calendarNotes:
Orange County Public Schools begins the 2026-2027 school year on August 11, 2026. Spring Break runs March 15–19, 2027 — one week earlier than the three South Florida counties (Miami-Dade, Broward, Palm Beach), which all break March 22–26. The school year ends May 26, 2027. This one-week difference from South Florida is important for families planning intra-Florida travel: the week that Orlando families are on Spring Break, South Florida families are still in school, which means Orlando theme parks are somewhat less crowded than they will be the following week.

Orange County Public Schools serves the greater Orlando area, home to Walt Disney World, Universal Studios, SeaWorld, and ICON Park. However, the school calendar is not designed to avoid peak tourism seasons. OCPS Spring Break (March 15–19) falls during one of the highest-attendance weeks at Orlando's major theme parks, as Spring Break travelers from across the eastern United States arrive throughout March. Families planning a local theme park trip during Spring Break should expect wait times of 60–120+ minutes for major attractions. If local outings are the plan, scheduling them during the first week after Spring Break (March 22–29) — when South Florida schools are on break and Orlando schools are back — offers shorter park lines.

Orange County's tourism and hospitality workforce is one of the largest in the country, and a significant portion of OCPS families work in the theme park, hotel, and convention industries. School scheduling affects childcare and staffing across the local economy in ways that are not typical of non-tourism districts. Families whose work schedules change based on theme park operating hours or convention center bookings should plan school-year childcare arrangements early, as demand around school holidays is high.

Florida law permits districts to bank up to three emergency makeup days into the calendar for weather-related closures. Unlike South Florida, Central Florida's hurricane risk is moderate rather than severe — storms typically weaken as they cross the Florida peninsula before reaching Orlando. That said, OCPS has experienced closures from tropical storm effects, particularly flooding. Families booking late-May travel should consider whether any closures earlier in the year might have consumed the district's makeup day buffer.
